steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank completes what readers may conclude when they take the “autobiographical” label to mean Mitchell “merely fictionalized actual events.” The passage flags this as a drawback tied specifically to the “high value placed on creativity and imagination.” The correct answer must follow directly from that logic.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Black Swan Green is not as worthy of praise as it might have been if it had no basis in fact': if readers see the novel as constrained by real events rather than imaginatively created, they would conclude it deserves less praise given that creativity is highly valued. This follows directly from the passage's stated drawback. ✓
  • Choice B — 'critics disagree about whether Black Swan Green shows greater originality than works without autobiographical elements': the passage discusses reader perception, not critical disagreement. Nothing supports a claim about critics debating originality. ✗
  • Choice C — 'the real-world counterparts of other characters in Black Swan Green are hard to identify': this is about identifying sources for characters, which the passage never addresses. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Mitchell should not have claimed that Black Swan Green is based on real events': the passage says Mitchell is described by others as autobiographical—it never says Mitchell made that claim himself, and this conclusion is about responsibility rather than the creativity/value concern. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
